{{Quote|Ready for recon.|An AT-AR pilot|<ref name="Campaigns">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]''</ref>}}
The '''All Terrain Advance Raider (AT-AR)''', also known as the '''light AT-ST''' or simply '''scout walker''', was a variant of the [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transport]] used by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:AT-AR_cockpit.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A pair of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] in the cockpit of an AT-AR]]
A variant of the [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transport]],<ref name="TCSWE">{{CSWECite|I|22|All Terrain Advance Raider (AT-AR walker)}}</ref> the All Terrain Advance Raider had heavier armor than the AT-ST<ref name="Inside the Worlds of Star Wars Trilogy" /> for frontline combat.<ref name="Assault Team">''[[Star Wars: Assault Team]]''</ref> It was also smaller and faster, though only armed with a single [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]].<ref name="TCSWE" /> Its "head" had an angular viewport, while a blister was located on either side of the [[Walker/Legends|walker's]] head. The [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] sat one [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and one [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]] side-by-side.<ref name="The Bounty Hunter of Ord Mantell" />

A [[:File:Atarvariantcilpar.jpg|variant]] of the All Terrain Advance Raider, featuring additional weapons attached to its blisters and a body and legs resembling that of an AT-ST, saw service on [[Cilpar]],<ref name="The Rebel Opposition, Part 4">[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 4|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 4]]</ref> while another had relatively thin armor and went by the name light AT-ST.<ref name="Galactic Battlegrounds">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''</ref>

==History==
In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], AT-ARs were part an [[Imperial garrison base|Imperial garrison]] established in [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].<ref name="Inside the Worlds of Star Wars Trilogy" /> Other [[Garrison/Legends|garrisons]] also had AT-AR walkers in their arsenal, such as the one on the [[Jungle/Legends|jungle]] world<ref name="The Bounty Hunter of Ord Mantell" /> of [[Jarnollen]].<ref name="TCSWE" /> There, an AT-AR [[Mission to Jarnollen|pursued]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] after they were discovered by the local Imperial troops. The walker hunted them throughout the night and the next morning, losing track and coming across the rebels several times in the dense jungle. At the end of the chase, as it prepared to fire a clear shot, the walker was destroyed by the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']].<ref name="The Bounty Hunter of Ord Mantell" /> After the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] [[Evacuation of Yavin|abandoned]] its [[Great Temple/Legends|base]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], AT-ARs were used during the Empire's [[Reytha campaign|campaign]] on [[Reytha]]. They were outfitted at the retaken base in the planet's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Yeere]] before advancing with the troops to retake other areas.<ref name="Galactic Battlegrounds" />
[[File:Atarvariantcilpar.jpg|thumb|200x200px|An AT-AR variant on Cilpar.]]
In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], AT-ARs formed part of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Maximilian Veers/Legends|Maximilian Veers']] [[Blizzard Force/Legends|Blizzard Force]] and participated in the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]].<ref name="Inside the Worlds of Star Wars Trilogy" /> An AT-AR saw combat with the garrison on [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], where it faced opposition from [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] rebels led by [[Fenn Shysa]].<ref name="The Search Begins">[[Star Wars (1977) 68|''Star Wars'' (1977) 68]]</ref> During a [[mission to Cilpar]],<ref name="The Rebel Opposition, Part 4" /> an AT-AR<ref name="TCSWE" /> formed part of an Imperial convoy that carried [[Winter Celchu]], disguised at the time as Leia Organa, to a local base in capital city of [[Kiidan]].<ref name="The Rebel Opposition, Part 4" />

==Behind the scenes==
The All Terrain Advance Raider was originally created for the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] [[Star Wars (comic strip)|newspaper comic strip]] ''[[The Bounty Hunter of Ord Mantell]]'', written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and penciled by [[Al Williamson]], which ran from [[February 9]] to [[April 19]], [[1981]].<ref name="The Bounty Hunter of Ord Mantell" /> Before it was first identified in the [[2004]] reference book ''[[Inside the Worlds of Star Wars Trilogy]]'',<ref name="Inside the Worlds of Star Wars Trilogy" /> the [[2001]] [[Wikipedia:Real-time strategy|real time strategy]] game ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' referred to the AT-AR as the "[[:File:LightAT-ST.jpg|light AT-ST]]."<ref name="Galactic Battlegrounds" /> It was based on early concept art of the All Terrain Scout Transport (dubbed the "one-man scout walker") developed by [[Joe Johnston]] for [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']], and the [[All Terrain Defense Pod]] was derived from the same sketch.<ref name="Rebels Panel">{{YouTube|video=XWzg3Uu_H4Y|text=NYCC 2013: Star Wars Rebels Panel|nobackup=1}}</ref>
